commit.message:
Add real-time team dashboard for operational visibility

Problem: No visibility into who's working, who's idle, or who's ignoring messages.
Engineer had 7 unread critical messages for hours with no way to detect it.

Solution: Real-time dashboard showing:
- Team member status (working/idle/unresponsive)
- Current tasks for each person
- Unread message counts
- Activity timeline
- Task queue by owner
- Last activity timestamps

Features:
- Auto-refreshes every 30 seconds
- Color-coded status indicators (green/yellow/red)
- Flags unresponsive team members (idle + unread messages >30min)
- Shows recent message history per member
- Task queue visibility (in progress + pending)

Files:
- /app/dashboard/page.tsx - Main dashboard page
- /lib/team-status.ts - Core logic for reading team state
- /components/Header.tsx - Added Dashboard nav link

commit.message:
Fix async arena engine to handle Sparring Partner and A2A JSON-RPC

The callAgent() function in engine.ts was sending plain JSON payloads
but agents expect A2A JSON-RPC 2.0 format. Also wasn't handling the
Sparring Partner internally (tried to HTTP fetch instead of calling
handleSparringRequest directly). Ported the working logic from the
sync fight route: SP calls go direct, external agents get JSON-RPC
with SSRF protection and A2A response extraction.

commit.message:
v0.9 — Retrieval intelligent: TF-IDF + scoring Generative Agents + auto-segmentation

3 briques ajoutees au moteur:

1. TF-IDF retrieval dans boot() — remplace le tag matching basique
   par cosine similarity sur le contenu reel des branches .mn.
   Zero dependance (math + Counter), Python pur.

2. Scoring Generative Agents (Park et al. 2023) dans boot():
   score = 0.2*recency + 0.2*importance + 0.6*relevance
   Recency = decay lineaire 90j, importance = log(access_count),
   relevance = TF-IDF cosine similarity avec la query.

3. Auto-segmentation dans feed_from_hook():
   grow_branches_from_session() decoupe les .mn compresses en branches
   thematiques dans l'arbre. Merge si >50% overlap de tags.
   Fallback chunking si pas de ## headers.

Teste: "binance trading" -> branches HSBC, "scan glyph" -> branches Ygg.
L'arbre grossit automatiquement a chaque session.

commit.message:
Audit resolution: 100% bilateral traceability, failure mode formalization, V1 audit finalization

Completes the V1 Fagan Inspection audit cycle. ADRS.md achieves 154/154 forward
traceability. FAILURE_MODES.md gains formal violation predicates. Audit triage and
divergence matrix reflect all resolution sessions through Session 014.

=== ADRS.md (154/154 forward annotations) ===

Achieves 100% bilateral traceability between the design decision index and the
formal specification. Every entry now has a "Formalized across" annotation linking
to specific INV and ADR IDs in spec/.

Key updates:
- ~20 entries expanded from single-ADR references to multi-element references
  spanning namespaces (e.g., FD-001 now references both ADR-STORE-001 and
  ADR-TRILATERAL-002)
- SQ-012 renumbering (was duplicate SQ-011)
- Lifecycle model entries (LM-010) linked to ADR-TRILATERAL-001/003
- Coherence entries (CO-008, CO-010) linked to ADR-TRILATERAL-001
- Harvest warning (IB-012) linked to ADR-INTERFACE-010

This closes the V1 audit finding that ~8% of ADRS.md entries lacked forward links
to spec elements. The bilateral traceability chain is now complete:
  SEED.md → ADRS.md → spec/ (forward)
  spec/ → ADRS.md → SEED.md (backward, via :spec/traces-to)

=== FAILURE_MODES.md (formal violation predicates, 3 new entries) ===

Transforms the failure mode catalog from an observational list into a formally
testable corpus. Changes:

- Entry protocol expanded with two new steps: "formal violation predicate"
  (predicate logic expression) and "record observations with specific numbers"
- Existing entries FM-001 through FM-013 each gain:
  - "Why this is structurally hard for agents" analysis section
  - Formal violation predicate in predicate logic (machine-evaluable)
  - Concrete observations with specific numbers from audit sessions

Three new failure modes discovered during the V1 audit itself:
- FM-020: Autonomous Execution Without Authorization — agents deciding
  unilaterally on scope changes, simplifications, or architectural decisions
  without explicit user approval. Observed in 3 of 14 audit sessions.
- FM-021: Cascading Simplification Opacity — audit simplifications (e.g.,
  "Stage 0 stub datoms") applied across multiple spec sections without
  consolidated user review of their aggregate effect. The D1 Simplification
  Detail table in V1_AUDIT_TRIAGE.md was created retroactively to address this.
- FM-022: Scope Confusion Masquerading as Contradiction — the A4 finding
  (NEG-SCHEMA-001 vs ADR-SCHEMA-005) was initially classified as a true
  logical contradiction (P ∧ ¬P) but was actually a scope confusion (Schema
  has no state-box of its own ≠ Schema has no state). Distinguishing genuine
  contradictions from scope mismatches requires explicit scope annotation.

=== audits/stage-0/V1_AUDIT_TRIAGE.md ===

Updated to post-Session-014 final state:
- ADRS traceability: "~92% formalized" → "100% formalized (post-Session-013)"
- A4 resolution corrected: "Schema borrowed, not owned" → "Store owns Schema
  internally (Option C, ADR-SCHEMA-005)" — the first-principles review
  concluded that Schema is a derived view of store datoms, not a separate
  component with independent state.
- D3 resolution corrected: "60+ minutes with incremental caching" → "three-tier
  pipeline (5a/5b/5c)" reflecting ADR-VERIFICATION-001
- Traceability expanded: backward 120/120 + forward 154/154 = bilateral complete
- D1 Simplification Detail table added retroactively documenting all 8
  simplification notes with risk assessments, corrections applied, and ADR refs
  (addresses FM-021)
- Total spec elements: 238 → 295 (+62) across all sessions
- A4r user review decision recorded
- Multiple B/E/H category divergences marked FIXED (B2, B5, B6, B7, B9, B10,
  E3, H4)

=== audits/stage-0/divergence-resolution-matrix.md ===

Eight previously-REMAINING divergences marked FIXED:
  B2  (Clause variants)         — resolved in R6.7b
  B5  (QueryResult fields)      — resolved in R6.7b
  B6  (QueryExpr enum vs struct)— resolved in R6.7b
  B7  (QueryMode variant form)  — resolved in R6.7b
  B9  (CandidateStatus)         — resolved in R6.7b
  B10 (AssembledContext)         — resolved in R6.7b
  E3  (ParsedQuery/FindSpec)    — resolved in R6.7b
  H4  (graph algorithm returns) — resolved in R6.7b

Evidence: 74/76 complete (2 pending hardware proof)

Fix auto-range compressor: normalize by smoothed max density

remapping min-max range. This ensures the peak density always maps to

adaptation, and applied gain transform to stats to match display space.

fix: robust grade range validation (1.0–5.0) in Alpine.js

- Remove min/max HTML attributes (browser sanitizes input.value to ''
  for out-of-range numbers, hiding the value from the @input handler)
- Keep invalid value visible in the input (red border) instead of
  clearing it, so user sees exactly what needs to be corrected
- Replace hasErrors() with anyError() using Object.values().some()
  for reliable Alpine reactivity detection
- save() also validates notas directly (no dependency on notasError)
  so the block works even if reactive tracking has edge cases
- Server-side guardar() remains as final safety net

commit.message:
Fix Docker build: use npm ci --force to bypass platform check

--ignore-platform is not a valid npm install CLI flag on npm 10.
Use --force which overrides all platform compatibility checks,
allowing the Linux container to skip the Windows-only
@tailwindcss/oxide-win32-x64-msvc package.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Implementation guide: LAYOUT + TRILATERAL build plans, CRDT cascade fix, EDN format, type catalog closure

Updates all 12 existing guide files and adds 3 new namespace build plans to reflect
the expanded specification (83 Stage 0 INVs across 11 namespaces). Three major
themes: new namespace implementation blueprints, CRDT correctness propagation from
spec to guide, and phantom type closure in the type catalog.

=== New guide files ===

guide/01b-storage-layout.md (782 lines):
  Complete build plan for the LAYOUT namespace (11 INV, 7 ADR, 5 NEG). Defines
  the Store–Layout isomorphism split across two crate boundaries: pure
  serialization/hashing in braid-kernel/src/layout.rs and filesystem I/O in
  braid/src/persistence.rs. Covers canonical serialization (INV-LAYOUT-001),
  transaction hashing (INV-LAYOUT-002), content-addressed file naming
  (INV-LAYOUT-003), directory-union merge (INV-LAYOUT-007), and integrity
  self-verification (INV-LAYOUT-009). Full three-box (Mills cleanroom)
  decompositions for 5 functions.

guide/13-trilateral.md (349 lines):
  Build plan for the TRILATERAL namespace (6 Stage 0 INVs). Defines AttrNamespace
  enum (Intent/Spec/Impl/Meta), LiveView struct with datoms/entity_count/namespace
  fields, and public API: classify_attribute(), compute_phi(), formality_level(),
  live_intent/live_spec/live_impl(). All pure computations in a single
  braid-kernel/src/trilateral.rs module. Full three-box decompositions for 5
  functions with proptest strategies.

guide/10b-budget.md (34 lines):
  Stage 1 placeholder for the BUDGET namespace. Documents Stage 0 prep items
  (TokenCounter trait, ApproxTokenCounter, ADR-BUDGET-004) and deferred scope.

=== CRDT cascade determinism (guide/07-merge-basic.md) ===

Substantial rewrite (~80 lines) propagating INV-MERGE-010 from spec to guide:
- merge_cascade() renamed to run_cascade() with signature change from
  (&mut Store, &[Datom], AgentId) to (&Store, &[Datom]) — removing AgentId
  enforces determinism by construction (the cascade cannot depend on who
  detected the conflict).
- Cascade datom identity derived from content (conflict entity/attribute/values),
  not detection metadata.
- Steps 2-5 simplified to stub datoms per ADR-MERGE-007.
- New proptest for INV-MERGE-010 verifying merge(A,B) == merge(B,A).

=== Causal independence conflict detection (guide/04-resolution.md) ===

Rewrites has_conflict() from simple "identical TxId" check to proper causal
independence analysis that walks the causal_predecessors chain. Two assertions
conflict iff neither is a causal ancestor of the other (they are genuinely
concurrent). ~40 lines of Rust pseudocode with is_causal_ancestor() BFS.

=== Category 5 harvest strategy (guide/05-harvest.md) ===

Replaces the "Acknowledged Weakness" section (admitting ~30-40% knowledge loss)
with a three-mechanism progressive strategy:
1. Structured harvest prompt (Stage 0, ~60-70% capture rate)
2. Continuous externalization via dynamic CLAUDE.md (Stage 1-2, INV-HARVEST-009)
3. Fresh-agent Category 5 review (Stage 2, ADR-HARVEST-004)
Adds ExternalizationAnnotation struct and ingest_annotations() signature.

=== EDN interchange format (guide/00-architecture.md, guide/11-worked-examples.md) ===

- Architecture updated: layout.rs and trilateral.rs added to crate layout tree.
  Datom interchange format changed from JSONL to EDN-per-line (EDNL) per
  ADR-TRILATERAL-002. File extensions: .jsonl → .ednl.
- All worked examples converted from JSONL to EDN notation throughout
  ({"e":"blake3:..."} → {:e #blake3 "..."}). Namespace count updated 9 → 11.
  Stage 0 INV count updated 62 → 83 in all example outputs.

=== Verification guide (guide/10-verification.md) ===

- Gate 1 (V:TYPE): invariant list corrected with proper mappings
- Gate 2 (V:PROP): count updated 121/124 → 143/145
- Gate 3 (V:KANI): restructured into three-tier CI pipeline (5a/5b/5c)
  with harness count updated 27/41 → 32/48
- Gate 4 (V:MODEL): coverage updated 15 → 12 invariants
- New Harness 9 (Cascade Determinism) with full proptest for INV-MERGE-010
- Stage 0 total updated 64 → 83 throughout

=== Type catalog (guide/types.md) ===

Closes multiple phantom type issues (FM-013 Pattern P3):
- LAYOUT namespace: LayoutError enum, Blake3Hash newtype
- TRILATERAL namespace: AttrNamespace enum, LiveView struct (D-2 remediation:
  corrected from {entities: BTreeSet, datom_count} to {datoms: Vec, entity_count,
  namespace} matching guide/13-trilateral.md)
- SchemaValidationError: full enum definition (was [SPEC-ONLY])
- AssociateCue: Semantic + Explicit variants with depth/breadth (was [UNDEFINED])
- DeliberationStatus: rewritten with partial order semantics, join(:stalled,
  :decided) = :contested, custom PartialOrd (must NOT derive Ord)
- MCPServer/BraidMcpServer: Arc<Store> → ArcSwap<Store> (Datomic connection model)
- Schema concurrency note: MVCC snapshot model explanation

=== Other guide updates ===

- guide/02-schema.md: Schema and MVCC Snapshots section (ADR-STORE-016)
- guide/06-seed.md: AssociateCue enum definition with depth=3, breadth=10 defaults
- guide/08-guidance.md: M(t) weight bootstrap source note
- guide/12-stages-1-4.md: All stage INV counts updated, LAYOUT+TRILATERAL added
- guide/README.md: Build order expanded 9 → 11 steps, Stage 0 scope 62 → 83 INVs,
  Kani CI description updated for three-tier pipeline

- 過去記事参照を「先週」→「前に」「以前」に変更

Prevent data loss when downstream projects have project-specific content
in shared files (.mcp.json, .pre-commit-config.yaml, skills, hooks).

commit.message:
Fix avatar upload by routing through server-side API with service role key

The client-side Supabase storage upload was failing due to missing RLS
policies on the avatars bucket. Added a /api/upload endpoint that validates
auth and uploads using the service role key.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix search button overflow + auto-enable pensia-net for pension type

- Portfolio search button: add align-items:center, flex-shrink:0, min-width:0 to fix RTL overflow
- Pension type: auto-check isPensiaTracked and show pensiaFields on type select (no manual checkbox)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Cross-pollinate web4 sessions 30-31: evidence weights, trust invariants, Byzantine detection

Trust Tensor: Evidence strength hierarchy (hardware 1.0, direct 0.9, attestation
0.7, delegation 0.5, gossip 0.3) with visual bar chart. Explains why MRH caps
trust at 3 hops.

How It Works: Four trust invariants (boundedness, conservation, transitivity
bounds, locality) in collapsible detail. 176 formal checks.

Threat Model: Byzantine detection section — equivocation detection, behavioral
fingerprinting, gradual degradation vs blockchain slashing. 85 formal checks.

Source: web4 sessions 30-31 (trust_topology_invariants.py,
trust_evidence_chains.py, byzantine_fault_detection.py)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add benchmark report 2026-03-07: ~1000 TPS clean, 1849 TPS keep-alive

Measured with apache benchmark (ab) from 2-vCPU general node via NLB.
Encryption-only latency 0.024ms avg / 0.038ms max (84,120 samples from
CloudWatch EMF). Test-client CPU is the binding constraint at c=50+.
